model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 14","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_BATCHNORM = True","old":"USE_BATCHNORM = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The previous change likely over-expanded the MLP and decayed the learning rate too aggressively over only 256 steps. This candidate returns to a shorter network with BatchNorm, keeps only a modest width increase, and switches to AdamW with slightly stronger regularization for better short-budget optimization.","secondary_modes":["layout","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 7e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, the current run only sees a small fraction of CIFAR-10. Doubling batch size should substantially increase sample coverage within the same step budget, and a modest learning-rate increase keeps optimization progress from slowing as the batch gets larger.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The large gain from moving to batch 128 suggests sample coverage is the main bottleneck under fixed-step evaluation. Pushing to batch 256 should let the model cover roughly a full epoch within 256 steps, and a moderate learning-rate increase keeps AdamW effective at the larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The current configuration is now strong enough that optimization is likely limited by end-of-run refinement rather than raw coverage. Keeping the same successful batch size and optimizer while adding cosine decay over the fixed 256-step horizon is a low-complexity way to preserve fast early progress and reduce late-step overshooting.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)\n","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()\n","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The current setup already improved optimization and coverage substantially. A small amount of label smoothing is a low-complexity regularization change that often reduces validation cross-entropy directly by preventing overconfident logits, which matches the verifier objective better than chasing more raw accuracy alone.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()\n","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)\n","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Label smoothing helped less than expected, so this candidate restores the stronger plain cross-entropy setup and continues along the sample-coverage direction that has been consistently improving results. Moving from batch 256 to 384 should push the fixed 256-step run closer to two full effective passes over CIFAR-10, with a small learning-rate increase to match the larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 384","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest gains so far have come from increasing sample coverage under the fixed 256-step budget. Pushing batch size to 512 should expose the model to substantially more data within the same horizon, and a small learning-rate increase keeps the effective update scale aligned with the larger batch while preserving the successful optimizer and cosine schedule setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 14","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimization side is now working well: larger effective coverage, AdamW, and cosine decay have all helped. A small width increase is the next low-complexity capacity test, giving the flat MLP a bit more representational power without changing the successful training loop or making the architecture much deeper.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The main source of improvement has still been increased sample coverage within the fixed 256-step budget, and the move to batch 512 continued to help. This candidate tests whether that trend extends further by pushing batch size to 768 while slightly increasing the learning rate to keep update magnitude aligned with the larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The dominant positive trend is still larger-batch training under the fixed 256-step verifier horizon. Moving to batch 1024 should push effective data exposure even further, and a small accompanying learning-rate increase keeps optimization speed aligned with the larger batch while leaving the otherwise successful setup intact.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The improvement curve from larger batches is still strong, which suggests the fixed 256-step budget remains dominated by sample coverage rather than optimizer saturation. This candidate continues that trend with a larger batch and a proportional learning-rate increase while keeping the rest of the now-successful AdamW plus cosine configuration unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1536","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Larger batches have remained the most reliable improvement axis, and the current configuration still improved when moving to 1536. This candidate tests whether that trend continues by pushing sample coverage further with batch 2048, paired with a modest learning-rate increase to keep optimization speed matched to the larger batch under the same fixed 256-step horizon.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Batch size increases have carried the run far, so the current bottleneck may be underfitting rather than insufficient data exposure. With AdamW, cosine decay, and very large effective coverage now in place, reducing weight decay is a low-complexity way to let the MLP fit more aggressively without disturbing the successful large-batch training recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 3072","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The weight-decay change was neutral, while the consistent positive signal across the run has come from larger effective batch sizes under the fixed 256-step budget. This candidate follows that strongest trend again by increasing batch size substantially and slightly raising the learning rate so the optimization scale stays matched to the larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 3072","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The strongest and most consistent signal in this run is that increasing effective batch size under the fixed 256-step budget keeps lowering validation loss. This candidate continues along that same axis with a moderate step up to batch 4096 and a small learning-rate increase so the optimizer stays matched to the larger batch.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The warmup-plus-cosine schedule unlocked a much better regime, which suggests the previous base learning rate may now be conservative. A small increase is a focused next test that preserves the successful optimizer, batch size, and scheduler shape while probing whether the model can make even stronger progress within the same 256-step horizon.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 4.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 4","old":"WARMUP_EPOCHS = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The warmup-based scheduler was the strongest improvement so far, while the higher 5e-3 learning rate slightly regressed. This candidate restores the better base learning rate and tests whether a longer warmup phase can further stabilize large-batch optimization before the cosine decay takes over.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}
